Title: The apiosyltransferase celery UGT94AX1 catalyzes the biosynthesis of the flavone glycoside apiin

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 UDP-glycosyltransferase (UGT94 family); glycoside-specific glycosyltransferase (GGT); PSPG motif-containing protein
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Apiin biosynthesis / apiosylated flavone glycoside biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Terminal glycosylation step of flavone 7-O-glucoside apiosylation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers an apiosyl residue from UDP-apiose to the 2-position of the glucose moiety of flavone 7-O-glucosides, converting apigenin 7-O-glucoside to apiin and also apiosylating other flavone/flavonol 7-O-glucosides.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: True leaves; also detected in seeds
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes apiin biosynthesis by catalyzing the last sugar modification step; likely produces multiple apiose-containing flavone glycosides in celery.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: terminal determinant; expression-correlated control step, but strict rate-limiting status not formally established
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT94AX1 (Agr35256-1) was identified by coexpression and phylogenetic screening as the strongest candidate apiin-biosynthetic apiosyltransferase in celery. 2. Recombinant UGT94AX1 converted apigenin 7-O-glucoside plus UDP-apiose into apiin in vitro, demonstrating the last step of apiin biosynthesis. 3. The enzyme showed strict sugar donor specificity for UDP-apiose and accepted several flavone/flavonol 7-O-glucosides as acceptors. 4. Ile139, Phe140, and Leu356 are crucial for UDP-apiose recognition and full activity. 5. Sequence and phylogenetic analyses suggest UGT94AX1 is the sole apiosyltransferase-encoding gene in the celery genome.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in early leaf development; peaks at true leaf developmental stage 2; high in true leaves and low in stems, roots, and seeds
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in early true leaves, peaking at stage 2, and reduced in later stages; high in true leaves, low in stems, roots, and seeds
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq candidate screening; RT-qPCR expression profiling; recombinant protein enzyme assay; HPLC product identification with authentic apiin; kinetic analysis; site-directed mutagenesis; homology modeling
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developmental stage of leaf growth; seedlings grown at 22°C under 16-h light/8-h dark cycle
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Low catalytic turnover (low kcat) despite high apiin accumulation; no organism-level growth tradeoff reported
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: AgApiT is an inverting glycosyltransferase. It has strict donor specificity for UDP-apiose, but moderate acceptor promiscuity toward flavone/flavonol 7-O-glucosides. The enzyme is inferred to be the only ApiT in celery. Key residues Ile139, Phe140, and Leu356 shape the donor pocket for apiose recognition and exclude UDP-Glc/UDP-Xyl.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Upstream flavone aglycone formation supplying apiin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes flavone formation in the upstream pathway leading to apigenin, the aglycone precursor of apiin.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: True leaves; also detected in seeds
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports apiin accumulation by providing the flavone precursor pathway and is coexpressed with UGT94AX1 during early leaf development.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AgFNSI expression pattern parallels UGT94AX1 during celery leaf development. 2. High AgFNSI expression in early true leaves is consistent with a role in supplying the apigenin precursor for apiin biosynthesis. 3. The paper uses AgFNSI as a known apiin biosynthesis-related enzyme for coexpression support rather than new biochemical characterization.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High in early leaf developmental stages; similar tissue/developmental pattern to UGT94AX1; high in true leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in early leaf developmental stages and true leaves; low in other tissues
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR expression profiling correlated with apiin accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included in Results as a known apiin biosynthesis-related enzyme gene used for coexpression comparison; not newly functionally characterized here.
